edges.description: Loss of the beta subunit reduces total beta-hexosaminidase activity.
Combined Hex A and Hex B deficiency is reflected by low total beta-hexosaminidase activity in patient serum.
Impaired ganglioside catabolism drives lysosomal storage of GM2, with secondary GA2 buildup in disease models.
edges.target: Lysosomal GM2 and GA2 accumulation in neural cells

edges.description: Systemic lysosomal storage from combined beta-hexosaminidase deficiency can include coarse facial morphology in some Sandhoff patients.
The lysosomal storage lesion is directly measurable as increased GM2 gangliosides.
edges.source: Lysosomal GM2 and GA2 accumulation in neural cells

edges.description: GA2 glycolipid accumulation is a related storage readout in Sandhoff disease models.
edges.source: Lysosomal GM2 and GA2 accumulation in neural cells

edges.description: Storage distends and injures lysosomes across multiple neural cell types.
edges.source: Lysosomal GM2 and GA2 accumulation in neural cells
edges.target: Lysosomal dysfunction in neurons and glia

edges.description: Neural glycosphingolipid storage includes retinal involvement that manifests clinically as cherry-red macular spots.
intermediate_mechanisms: Retinal ganglion-cell storage makes the surrounding retina pale while the fovea remains red.
edges.source: Lysosomal GM2 and GA2 accumulation in neural cells

edges.description: Abnormal lysosomal compartments are associated with activated glia and inflammatory pathology.
edges.source: Lysosomal dysfunction in neurons and glia

edges.description: Chronic lysosomal stress is linked to downstream ER stress in vulnerable spinal motor neurons.
intermediate_mechanisms: Lysosomal storage perturbs neuronal proteostasis and organelle stress responses.
edges.source: Lysosomal dysfunction in neurons and glia
edges.target: ER stress in spinal motor neurons

edges.description: Reactive gliosis accompanies and likely amplifies progressive neural tissue injury.
intermediate_mechanisms: Activated microglia and astrocytes amplify inflammatory and degenerative injury programs in neural tissue.

edges.description: Persistent ER stress is accompanied by downstream apoptotic signaling in vulnerable motor neurons.
edges.source: ER stress in spinal motor neurons

edges.description: Motor neuron apoptosis contributes to lower motor neuron disease and overall neurologic decline.
Progressive neurodegeneration disrupts developmental maintenance and leads to developmental arrest or regression in infantile Sandhoff disease.
intermediate_mechanisms: Progressive neuronal loss and circuit failure impair retained developmental skills.

edges.description: Circuit dysfunction in infantile GM2 gangliosidosis commonly manifests as exaggerated startle.
intermediate_mechanisms: Brainstem and auditory startle-circuit dysfunction increases startle responsiveness.

edges.description: Neurodegenerative motor-circuit dysfunction contributes to truncal hypotonia and muscle weakness.
intermediate_mechanisms: Central and lower motor neuron dysfunction reduce tone and motor control.

edges.description: Progressive cortical and network dysfunction can lower seizure threshold in infantile Sandhoff disease.
intermediate_mechanisms: Neurodegenerative network instability increases epileptic susceptibility.

edges.target: Lysosomal GM2 and GA2 accumulation in neural cells
ER stress in spinal motor neurons

edges.description: Decreased total Hex A + B activity reports the combined beta-hexosaminidase deficiency caused by HEXB pathogenic variants.
Increased GM2 ganglioside storage directly reports the core lysosomal storage lesion.

edges.description: GA2 accumulation tracks the glycosphingolipid storage burden in experimental Sandhoff disease.

nodes.description: 4-PBA is a preclinical pharmacologic strategy aimed at ER-stress-associated neuronal injury in Sandhoff disease.

evidence.reference_title: 4-Phenylbutyric acid mitigates ER stress-induced neurodegeneration in the spinal cords of a GM2 gangliosidosis mouse model.
evidence.snippet: Our studies also tested the impact of the chemical chaperone 4-PBA on ER stress in mice, and following administration, we observed significant improvements in motor neuromuscular function and life span throughout disease progression.

evidence.reference_title: 4-Phenylbutyric acid mitigates ER stress-induced neurodegeneration in the spinal cords of a GM2 gangliosidosis mouse model.
evidence.snippet: 4-PBA treatment significantly reduced apoptosis in spinal cord neurons and increased the number of choline acetyltransferase (ChAT)-positive neurons, with little effect on astrogliosis or sensory interneurons.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Molecular Characteristics of Two Chinese Children with Infantile Sandhoff Disease and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Infantile Sandhoff disease is an autosomal recessive inherited disease primarily characterized by cherry red spots in the retina, muscle weakness, seizure, truncal hypotonia, hyperacusis, developmental delay and regression.

evidence.reference_title: Sandhoff Disease without Hepatosplenomegaly Due to Hexosaminidase B Gene Mutation.
evidence.snippet: The child had coarse facies without hepatosplenomegaly.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Molecular Characteristics of Two Chinese Children with Infantile Sandhoff Disease and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Infantile Sandhoff disease is an autosomal recessive inherited disease primarily characterized by cherry red spots in the retina, muscle weakness, seizure, truncal hypotonia, hyperacusis, developmental delay and regression.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of infantile G(M2) gangliosidosis.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: Common symptoms at onset were developmental arrest (83%), startling (65%), and hypotonia (60%).

nodes.description: In Sandhoff mouse spinal cord, chronic ER stress markers rise within motor neurons, linking lysosomal disease stress to downstream lower motor neuron injury.
nodes.id: ER stress in spinal motor neurons

evidence.reference_title: 4-Phenylbutyric acid mitigates ER stress-induced neurodegeneration in the spinal cords of a GM2 gangliosidosis mouse model.
evidence.snippet: We analyzed the expression and localization of ER stress and cellular apoptosis markers, which revealed significant upregulation of these factors within motor neurons.

evidence.reference_title: Sandhoff Disease without Hepatosplenomegaly Due to Hexosaminidase B Gene Mutation.
evidence.snippet: A 1-year-old male child presented with regression of milestones, exaggerated startle response, decreased vision, and seizures from 6 months of age.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of infantile G(M2) gangliosidosis.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: Common symptoms at onset were developmental arrest (83%), startling (65%), and hypotonia (60%).

evidence.reference_title: Brain endothelial specific gene therapy improves experimental Sandhoff disease.
evidence.snippet: They improved neurological function and reduced accumulation of the ganglioside GM2 and the glycolipid GA2 as well as astrocytic activation.

readouts.interpretation: GA2 accumulation tracks the glycosphingolipid storage burden in experimental Sandhoff disease.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Molecular Characteristics of Two Chinese Children with Infantile Sandhoff Disease and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: The pathogenic genetic defects of the HEXB gene, which encodes the β subunit of the hexosaminidase A (ɑβ) and hexosaminidase B (ββ) enzymes, cause deficiency of both the Hex A and Hex B enzymes, resulting in the deposition of GM2 ganglion glycerides in the lysosomes of the central nervous system and somatic cells.

evidence.reference_title: 4-Phenylbutyric acid mitigates ER stress-induced neurodegeneration in the spinal cords of a GM2 gangliosidosis mouse model.
evidence.snippet: Sandhoff disease (SD), a fatal and rare lysosomal storage disorder (LSD), is caused by a deficiency of the enzyme β-hexosaminidase B and leads to severe accumulation of GM2 gangliosides in lysosomes, primarily within the central nervous system (CNS).

readouts.interpretation: Increased GM2 ganglioside storage directly reports the core lysosomal storage lesion.
readouts.target: Lysosomal GM2 and GA2 accumulation in neural cells

nodes.description: Investigational AAV-mediated HEXB replacement, or coordinated HEXA/HEXB delivery, has shown strong preclinical rescue of enzyme activity, storage pathology, gliosis, survival, and motor function in Sandhoff mouse models.

evidence.reference_title: Intravenous administration of scAAV9-Hexb normalizes lifespan and prevents pathology in Sandhoff disease mice.
evidence.snippet: Importantly, we demonstrate for the first time that this treatment results in a normal lifespan (over 700 days) and normalizes motor function assessed by a battery of behavioral tests, with scAAV9-treated SD mice being indistinguishable from wild-type littermates.

evidence.reference_title: Intravenous administration of scAAV9-Hexb normalizes lifespan and prevents pathology in Sandhoff disease mice.
evidence.snippet: AAV9 treatment was sufficient to prevent GM2 and GA2 storage almost completely in the cerebrum (less so in the cerebellum), as well as thalamic reactive gliosis and thalamocortical neuron loss in treated Hexb-/- mice.

evidence.reference_title: Brain endothelial specific gene therapy improves experimental Sandhoff disease.
evidence.snippet: Overall, the data demonstrate that endothelial cells are a suitable target for intravenous gene therapy of GM2 gangliosidoses and possibly other lysosomal storage disorders.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Molecular Characteristics of Two Chinese Children with Infantile Sandhoff Disease and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: The pathogenic genetic defects of the HEXB gene, which encodes the β subunit of the hexosaminidase A (ɑβ) and hexosaminidase B (ββ) enzymes, cause deficiency of both the Hex A and Hex B enzymes, resulting in the deposition of GM2 ganglion glycerides in the lysosomes of the central nervous system and somatic cells.

evidence.reference_title: Intravenous administration of scAAV9-Hexb normalizes lifespan and prevents pathology in Sandhoff disease mice.
evidence.snippet: A mouse model of Hexb deficiency reproduces the key pathognomonic features of SD patients with severe ubiquitous lysosomal dysfunction, GM2 accumulation, neuroinflammation and neurodegeneration, culminating in death at 4 months.

evidence.reference_title: Hexb enzyme deficiency leads to lysosomal abnormalities in radial glia and microglia in zebrafish brain development.
evidence.snippet: Hexb deficient zebrafish (hexb-/- ) showed lysosomal abnormalities already early in development both in radial glia, which are the neuronal and glial progenitors, and in microglia.

nodes.description: Progressive neuronal loss drives severe neurologic impairment, motor decline, and early death in Sandhoff disease.

evidence.reference_title: 4-Phenylbutyric acid mitigates ER stress-induced neurodegeneration in the spinal cords of a GM2 gangliosidosis mouse model.
evidence.snippet: This accumulation results in severe neurological impairment, lower motor neuron disease, and death.

evidence.reference_title: Hexb enzyme deficiency leads to lysosomal abnormalities in radial glia and microglia in zebrafish brain development.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, at 5 days postfertilization, hexb-/- zebrafish showed reduced locomotor activity.

nodes.description: Microglial activation and astroglial reactivity are prominent tissue-level consequences of ganglioside storage in Sandhoff disease.

nodes.description: Sandhoff disease is the HEXB-related form of GM2 gangliosidosis. Biallelic pathogenic variants in HEXB disrupt the beta subunit that participates in both hexosaminidase A and hexosaminidase B.
